title: WorkshopLM why-first beat cut

music.title: Different Window
music.editRule: The full 2:39 master is the edit skeleton. Every major section lands on its detected beat grid; the output range uses hard cuts on two- or four-beat phrases; the final picture resolves with the song tail.

researchRules: Start with the real problem and audience before naming the product.
Spend most of the runtime showing the working product, not describing it.
Keep the narration conversational and specific; no slide-deck pacing.
Use one visual idea per beat and hard cuts for the range/proof sequence.
State Codex and GPT-5.6 use explicitly and show evidence for both.
Return to the opening visual idea at the close so the film feels resolved.

shots.title: The work breaks apart after the meeting
shots.narration: The meeting ends. The documents are real. The decisions are real. Then the work splinters: slides in one tool, notes in another, graphics somewhere else, and no clean way back to the source.
shots.visual: Animated fragments of one professional project drift into disconnected tools; no product UI yet.

shots.title: One grounded Workshop
shots.narration: WorkshopLM keeps that work together. One conversation becomes one grounded Workshop, with the knowledge, visual direction, and source trail still attached.
shots.visual: Fragments converge into the WorkshopLM shell and the Capture → Map → Brief → Create spine.

shots.title: Capture the real starting material
shots.narration: Start by talking through the messy idea, pasting notes, adding a website, or importing a document. The original material stays local, visible, and selected inside the Workshop.
shots.visual: Live founder-authorized Capture/source state, cropped for legibility.

shots.title: GPT-5.6 Terra shapes the Map
shots.narration: GPT-5.6 Terra turns that material into a Map. Evidence becomes synthesis. Synthesis becomes one recommended direction. This is still an editable thinking surface, not a frozen model answer, and every grounded idea keeps its exact source locator.
shots.visual: Map hierarchy in motion with Evidence → Synthesis → Recommended direction called out visually.

shots.title: Every claim keeps its source
shots.narration: Open a Source link and WorkshopLM shows the sentence behind the claim. There is no separate citations page to hunt through. Change the evidence later, and affected work marks itself Needs update.
shots.visual: Source inspector and exact excerpt; then a compact Needs update state.

shots.title: Two human checkpoints
shots.narration: There are two real checkpoints. Approve the Brief before creating anything downstream. Approve the Storyboard before rendering Video. If the thinking changes, both gates fail closed until you review it again.
shots.visual: Brief approval and Storyboard approval land as two clear beats, not a form walkthrough.

shots.title: One direction, seven expressions
shots.narration: Then one approved direction becomes a Presentation, Infographic, image set, Audio Overview, Sketch, Storyboard, and Video. The formats are different, but they share one Style, one decision, and one source trail. Editable PowerPoint exports stay part of the package.
shots.visual: Fast hard-cut range proof: each output gets a distinct full-frame composition on the beat.

shots.title: Editable Storyboard to local Video
shots.narration: The Storyboard stays editable panel by panel: visual, narration, timing, and Source. After approval, HyperFrames renders the Video locally and records the claim, image, narration, and source hashes behind every scene.
shots.visual: Storyboard panels assemble, then the provider-backed Video plays inside a clean production frame.

shots.title: How Codex and GPT-5.6 were used
shots.narration: Codex with GPT-5.6 built and tested WorkshopLM. Inside the product, GPT-5.6 Terra turns the authorized Source into the grounded Map shown here.
shots.visual: Codex doorway, build log correction, and the verified Terra provenance record.

shots.title: The submission proves the idea
shots.narration: This submission started as one authorized conversation. WorkshopLM turned it into the work you just saw, with every claim still connected to its source.
shots.visual: Pull back from the final traced package to the opening fragments, now connected inside one Workshop.
